summary: Tested app-wide permission enforcement for viewer and owner roles. All tests pass - navigation items and settings menu correctly filtered based on user permissions, and direct URL access properly redirects unauthorized users to dashboard.

viewer_nav_items.notes: Viewer sees only: Dashboard, Observations, Actions, My Tasks, Library. Correctly hidden: Definitions, Causal Engine

viewer_settings_menu.notes: Viewer sees only: Task Planner, Decision Engine, User Statistics, Feedback. Correctly hidden: Equipment Manager, Forms, User Management, Permissions, AI Usage

owner_nav_items.notes: Owner sees all 7 nav items: Dashboard, Definitions, Observations, Causal Engine, Actions, My Tasks, Library

owner_settings_menu.notes: Owner sees all 10 settings items including Equipment Manager, Form Designer, User Management, Permissions, AI Usage

owner_direct_url_access.notes: Owner can access /causal-engine, /forms, /equipment-manager directly without redirect

context_for_next_testing_agent: Permission enforcement is working correctly. PermissionsContext.js uses canAccessRoute() and canSeeNavItem() to filter navigation. Layout.js filters navItems and settingsMenuItems based on permissions. App.js ProtectedRoute redirects unauthorized users to /dashboard. Viewer role has read:false for investigations, forms, equipment, users, settings features.
